expectations.text: internalEdges contains svc→core (workspace:* protocol) and web→core (file:../core link) — both recognized despite the different protocols
the svc→test-kit devDependencies edge is present and flagged as dev-only (it must not be presented as an ordinary runtime edge)
edges use layer names from the provided list (core, svc, web, test-kit), not the scoped package names (@edge/core etc.)
the legacy layer (no package.json) appears in gaps as having no recognizable manifest — not silently skipped, and no edges invented for it
expectations.evidence: legacy gaps=["packages/legacy has no package.json — internal/external dependencies for the legacy layer cannot be mapped from manifests; src/index.js explicitly notes it was checked in without a manifest"], legacy edges=[]

expectations.text: externalDeps name the defining deps: fastify for svc, react for web, zod for core, vitest for test-kit
ordering identifies core as the provider consumed by svc and web (changes first); test-kit's provider role, if mentioned, is qualified as dev-only
expectations.evidence: ordering={"signal":"core is the only shared runtime foundation (fan-in from svc and web); test-kit is a dev-time leaf feeding svc; svc and web are independent of each other; legacy is fully disconnected","dispatchOrder":[["core","test-kit"],["svc","web"],["legacy"]],"notes":"Stages are parallelizable within each tier. core and test-kit have no internal deps and can run first in parallel. svc (needs core runtime + test-kit dev) and web (needs core runtime) follow in parallel. legacy has no detected edges in either direction, so its position is unconstrained — placed last only because it is unmappable, not because of a known dependency."}
